answer:
The ghazal is Mirza Ghalib's ghazal number 148 in *Diwan-E-Ghalib*.[^1]

**Opening paradox** (first couplet, first line):  
"When nothing was, then God would be; if nothing were, God still would be."[^2]  
Immediately followed by (first couplet, second line):  
"This state of being drowned me here; had I not been, what would there be?"[^2]

**Severed-head couplet** (second couplet):  
"So numb with grief, what should I dread if from my shoulders falls my head?  
Were it not severed, it would be laid gently, grieving, on my knee."[^3]

[^1]: Mirza Ghalib, *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, [chapter](/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/148-na-tha-kuch-toh-khuda-tha-kuch-na-hota-toh-khuda-h/translation/).
[^2]: Mirza Ghalib, *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, [paragraph](/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/148-na-tha-kuch-toh-khuda-tha-kuch-na-hota-toh-khuda-h/translation/#p-1420db).
[^3]: Mirza Ghalib, *Diwan-E-Ghalib*, [paragraph](/works/mirza-ghalib-diwan-e-ghalib-74ed4c/148-na-tha-kuch-toh-khuda-tha-kuch-na-hota-toh-khuda-h/translation/#p-6f69a2).

args.query: When nothing was, then God would be
had I not been, what would there be
